agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation in a logistics/courier system. A seemingly simple feature that intersects multiple bounded contexts: order management, dispatch, fulfillment, billing, and customer communication. The lifecycle stage of an order at cancellation time fundamentally changes what 'cancel' means operationally.","bounded_context":"Order Management — but with significant downstream effects on Dispatch, Fulfillment, Billing, and Notifications contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A customer's request for courier pickup and delivery of a parcel from origin to destination."},{"term":"Order Status","definition":"The current lifecycle stage of an order (e.g., Placed, Confirmed, Assigned, PickedUp, InTransit, Delivered, Cancelled)."},{"term":"Cancellation","definition":"A customer-initiated request to terminate an order before its completion. Not the same as a return or a refusal."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period during which cancellation is permitted without penalty or with reduced penalty, determined by how far the order has progressed."},{"term":"Cancellation Fee","definition":"A charge applied when cancellation occurs after resources have been committed (e.g., courier dispatched)."},{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver/rider assigned to fulfill the order."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The act of assigning a courier to an order and routing them toward pickup."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment to the customer upon successful cancellation, minus any applicable cancellation fee."},{"term":"Point of No Return","definition":"The order lifecycle stage beyond which cancellation is no longer permitted (typically once parcel is picked up or in transit)."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order may only be cancelled if it has not yet reached the Point of No Return (PickedUp status or beyond).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Once a courier has physical possession of a parcel, the system cannot unilaterally reverse that physical reality. Cancellation after pickup requires a different process (return/refusal). Allowing cancellation of a picked-up order would corrupt the fulfillment state.","testable_assertion":"Any cancellation request for an order with status in {PickedUp, InTransit, Delivered} MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A cancellation request must be idempotent — cancelling an already-cancelled order returns success without side effects.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Double-cancellation must not trigger duplicate refunds or duplicate courier notifications. This is a data consistency invariant.","testable_assertion":"Cancelling an order with status=Cancelled returns success and does not emit a second CancellationEvent or trigger a second refund."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Only the customer who placed the order (the order owner) may cancel it. Support agents may cancel on behalf of a customer through a separate internal process.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing any user to cancel any order is an authorization violation that corrupts order integrity.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request where requestor_id != order.customer_id AND requestor is not a support agent MUST be rejected with 403 Forbidden."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"If a courier has been assigned but has not yet picked up the parcel, cancellation is permitted but a cancellation fee MAY apply.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Whether a fee applies and its amount is a business policy decision that can be changed by operations leadership.","testable_assertion":"When an order with status=Assigned is cancelled, a CancellationFee (which may be zero) is recorded and the courier is notified to stand down."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Upon successful cancellation, a full refund minus any applicable cancellation fee must be initiated.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund policy (full, partial, credit) is a business decision. The current policy is full refund minus fee, but this could change.","testable_assertion":"When cancellation succeeds, a RefundInitiated event is emitted with amount = order.total - cancellation_fee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"An order in status Delivered or Completed cannot be cancelled. The customer must use the returns/disputes process instead.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A delivered order is a completed physical transaction. 'Cancelling' it is semantically meaningless — the domain concept is a return or dispute, not a cancellation. Conflating these corrupts the domain model.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request for an order with status in {Delivered, Completed} MUST be rejected with a message directing the user to the returns/disputes process."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Cancellation must atomically transition the order status to Cancelled and release all assigned resources (courier, route slot, etc.).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A partially cancelled order (status changed but courier still dispatched, or courier released but order still active) is a corrupted state that causes real-world operational chaos.","testable_assertion":"After cancellation, order.status=Cancelled AND no courier assignment exists for that order AND any reserved route slot is freed, all within a single transaction."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"The system must record who cancelled, when, and the order status at time of cancellation for audit purposes.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Cancellation audit trail is required for financial reconciliation and dispute resolution. Without it, refund fraud is undetectable.","testable_assertion":"Every successful cancellation creates a CancellationRecord with fields: order_id, cancelled_by, cancelled_at, previous_status, cancellation_fee_applied."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An order exists with status Placed or Confirmed (no courier assigned yet)","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled, a full refund is initiated with zero cancellation fee, and a CancellationRecord is persisted."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An order exists with status Assigned (courier dispatched but parcel not picked up)","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled, the courier is notified to stand down, any applicable cancellation fee is deducted, a refund of (order.total - cancellation_fee) is initiated, and the courier's route slot is released."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An order exists with status PickedUp or InTransit","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation request is rejected with a clear message explaining the order cannot be cancelled after pickup, and suggesting the returns/disputes process."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An order exists with status Delivered or Completed","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation request is rejected with a clear message directing the customer to the returns/disputes process. Order status remains unchanged."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An order exists with status Cancelled","when":"The order owner requests cancellation again","then":"The system returns success (idempotent), no duplicate refund is issued, no duplicate events are emitted, and no new CancellationRecord is created."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An order exists owned by Customer A","when":"Customer B (not a support agent) requests cancellation of that order","then":"The request is rejected with 403 Forbidden. The order status remains unchanged."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An order exists with status Assigned and a courier is en route","when":"The order owner requests cancellation and the cancellation succeeds","then":"The courier receives a real-time notification to abandon the pickup. The courier's availability is restored in the dispatch system."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A cancellation request is submitted but the payment refund service is temporarily unavailable","when":"The cancellation is processed","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled (the cancellation is committed), and the refund is queued for retry. The customer is informed that the refund is being processed. The cancellation is NOT rolled back due to refund failure."},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An order is in status Assigned","when":"The order owner requests cancellation at the exact moment the courier marks the parcel as picked up (race condition)","then":"Exactly one operation wins. If cancellation wins, BR-7 applies. If pickup wins, the cancellation is rejected per BR-1. The order is never left in an inconsistent state."},{"id":"AC-10","given":"Any successful cancellation occurs","when":"The cancellation is complete","then":"A CancellationRecord exists with order_id, cancelled_by, cancelled_at, previous_status, and cancellation_fee_applied. This record is immutable."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Race condition: customer cancels while courier simultaneously marks pickup","expected_behavior":"The system must use optimistic locking or equivalent to ensure exactly one state transition wins. If courier's pickup is recorded first, cancellation is rejected. If cancellation is recorded first, courier's pickup attempt fails and courier is notified the order was cancelled."},{"scenario":"Cancellation during payment processing (order just placed, payment still authorizing)","expected_behavior":"Cancellation should void the authorization rather than refund a capture. If capture already completed, proceed with standard refund flow."},{"scenario":"Customer loses connectivity mid-cancellation request","expected_behavior":"Cancellation must be atomic. Either it fully completes or it doesn't. A partial cancellation (status changed but no refund queued) must never occur. Client can retry safely due to idempotency."},{"scenario":"Multiple rapid cancellation requests from the same customer (double-click, retry)","expected_behavior":"First request processes the cancellation. Subsequent requests return success idempotently without duplicate side effects (no double refund, no duplicate courier notifications)."},{"scenario":"Order is in Assigned status but the courier has not yet acknowledged the assignment","expected_behavior":"Cancellation proceeds as normal for Assigned status. Courier assignment is revoked. If courier later tries to acknowledge, they receive an order-cancelled error."},{"scenario":"Cancellation fee calculation when promotional pricing or vouchers were applied","expected_behavior":"Cancellation fee is calculated on the base order economics, not on the discounted amount. Refund returns what the customer actually paid minus the cancellation fee. Vouchers/credits may have separate restoration rules."},{"scenario":"System outage: cancellation request arrives but database is unavailable","expected_behavior":"Request fails with a retryable error (503). No partial state changes. Customer is advised to retry."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels an order that is part of a batch/multi-parcel shipment","expected_behavior":"Only the specific order is cancelled, not the entire batch. Other orders in the batch continue unaffected. If the batch has shared routing, dispatch must re-optimize."},{"scenario":"Cancellation requested by a support agent on behalf of customer","expected_behavior":"Permitted, but the CancellationRecord must reflect cancelled_by as the support agent ID, not the customer. Cancellation fee policy may differ for agent-initiated cancellations (policy decision)."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellation directly impacts revenue (lost orders), refund processing costs, and potential cancellation fee revenue. Poor cancellation UX increases support call volume ($5-15 per call). Double-refund bugs from non-idempotent cancellation are direct financial losses. Overly restrictive cancellation policies increase chargebacks (which cost more than refunds).","operational":"Each cancellation of an Assigned order wastes courier time and fuel. The dispatch system must efficiently re-allocate freed couriers. High cancellation rates after assignment signal pricing or ETA problems upstream. Audit trail is critical for finance reconciliation.","user":"Cancellation is a trust feature — customers who know they CAN cancel are more likely to place orders. Friction in cancellation drives negative reviews and churn. Clear communication about cancellation windows and fees is essential for satisfaction. The point-of-no-return must be clearly communicated BEFORE and DURING ordering.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) race conditions between cancellation and pickup causing inconsistent state, (2) refund processing failures leaving customers unpaid, (3) authorization bypass allowing users to cancel others' orders, (4) missing audit trail enabling refund fraud. All are mitigable with proper design.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Gain ability to cancel orders, improving confidence and willingness to place orders. Must clearly understand when cancellation is/isn't possible and any fees."},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Will receive stand-down notifications for cancelled-after-assignment orders. Wasted trips must be minimized. Courier compensation policy for cancelled assignments needs definition."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Must reconcile cancellations, refunds, and cancellation fees. Require complete audit trail. Double-refund prevention is critical."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Reduced inbound volume for cancellation requests if self-service works well. Need tooling to cancel on behalf of customers for edge cases. Need visibility into cancellation history."},{"group":"Dispatch/Operations","impact":"Must handle real-time courier reassignment when assigned orders are cancelled. Cancellation rates become a key operational metric to monitor."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec is a single sentence with no definition of order lifecycle states, cancellation fees, refund policy, point-of-no-return, batch order handling, or courier compensation. Every business rule above is inferred from standard logistics domain knowledge, not from an actual spec. Multiple critical policy decisions (cancellation fee amounts, cancellation window boundaries, voucher restoration, courier compensation for wasted trips) are undefined. This analysis is a best-effort domain model that MUST be validated against the actual business requirements.","high_confidence_areas":["Order cannot be cancelled after physical pickup (universal logistics invariant)","Cancellation must be idempotent (fundamental system correctness)","Authorization — only order owner can cancel (universal security rule)","Audit trail requirement (financial/legal necessity)"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact cancellation fee policy and amounts","Whether cancellation is allowed during Assigned status or only before assignment","Voucher/promotion restoration rules on cancellation","Courier compensation policy for cancelled assignments","Batch/multi-parcel cancellation behavior","Whether support agents have different cancellation fee rules"],"assumptions":["Order lifecycle follows: Placed → Confirmed → Assigned → PickedUp → InTransit → Delivered → Completed","Point of no return is PickedUp (could be earlier or later depending on business)","Refund is monetary (not store credit)","Cancellation fee exists as a concept (may not apply in all cases)","Real-time courier notification capability exists"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define WHEN in the order lifecycle cancellation is permitted. Is the cutoff at courier assignment, courier pickup, or some other point? This is the single most important business decision for this feature.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Finance","Operations"],"options":["A: Allow cancellation only before courier assignment (strictest)","B: Allow cancellation before pickup, with fee after assignment (balanced)","C: Allow cancellation at any time before delivery (most permissive)"],"recommendation":"Option B — industry standard for courier services. Balances customer flexibility with operational efficiency."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No cancellation fee policy is defined. Should there be a fee? When does it apply? How much?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance"],"options":["A: No cancellation fee ever","B: Fee only after courier assigned","C: Sliding fee based on order progress"],"recommendation":"Option B — compensates for wasted courier resources without penalizing early cancellations."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No information about whether orders can be multi-parcel or part of batches. Cancellation behavior differs significantly for batch orders.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Dispatch/Operations"],"options":["A: Assume single-parcel orders only for MVP","B: Require batch cancellation rules before implementation"],"recommendation":"Option A for MVP, but flag batch handling as a fast-follow requirement."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Courier compensation policy for cancelled assignments is undefined. If a courier is en route to pickup and the order is cancelled, are they compensated for wasted time/fuel?","affected_stakeholders":["Couriers","Finance"],"options":["A: No courier compensation for cancellations","B: Flat compensation fee per cancelled assignment","C: Distance-based compensation"],"recommendation":"Must be defined before implementation — directly affects courier satisfaction and retention. Recommend escalating to Operations leadership."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation in a logistics/courier system. A seemingly simple feature that intersects multiple bounded contexts: order management, dispatch, fulfillment, billing, and customer communication. The lifecycle stage of an order at cancellation time fundamentally changes what 'cancel' means operationally.","bounded_context":"Order Management — but with significant downstream effects on Dispatch, Fulfillment, Billing, and Notifications contexts.","ubiquito
